parsed.summary: The cumulative v0.4.x diff deletes scripts/lib/process.mjs entirely (67 lines removed) while modifying multiple consumer modules. If any remaining import resolves that path at runtime, the result is an uncaught ModuleNotFoundError — a hard crash with no fallback. Separately, tracked-jobs.mjs sees 78 lines changed with no dedicated test file in the suite, leaving job-lifecycle regressions undetectable until production use. Both issues are consistent with the kind of silent failure that ships and then breaks under stress.

findings.title: process.mjs deleted entirely — potential dangling imports will crash at runtime
findings.body: scripts/lib/process.mjs (67 lines) was completely removed. At least glm-companion.mjs, render.mjs, state.mjs, job-control.mjs, and tracked-jobs.mjs were all modified in the same diff, but the repository summary does not confirm that every former consumer of process.mjs had its import statements removed. In ESM, a bare `import … from './lib/process.mjs'` that points to a deleted file throws ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND at load time — not at call time — so the entire plugin fails to register. This class of error is invisible in diff review and only manifests when the module graph is first resolved. No test file for process.mjs appears in the test directory either, meaning there is no automated guard that would have caught a surviving import.
findings.recommendation: Grep the entire codebase for any remaining reference to 'process.mjs' (including dynamic imports or re-exports). If none exist, add a CI check that verifies no source file imports a path that does not resolve. If consumers were updated, document that verification in the commit message or PR description so reviewers can confirm.

findings.title: tracked-jobs.mjs has 78 lines changed but no dedicated test coverage
findings.body: scripts/lib/tracked-jobs.mjs manages the lifecycle of background jobs — creation, state tracking, and presumably cleanup on exit or crash. The diff shows 78 lines changed in this module, yet no tracked-jobs.test.mjs appears anywhere in the test suite (the closest is job-render.test.mjs at 144 lines, which by name tests rendering, not job lifecycle). Job-tracking bugs manifest as orphaned processes, zombie state files, or lost work on shutdown. These are exactly the class of failure that is expensive to debug and hard to reproduce, and they are invisible without targeted tests for partial-failure and cleanup paths.
findings.recommendation: Add a tracked-jobs.test.mjs covering at minimum: (1) job creation and state persistence, (2) cleanup on explicit stop, (3) cleanup/recovery after simulated crash (unclean shutdown), (4) concurrent job tracking correctness. This should be a merge-blocker for any further changes to job lifecycle.

findings.title: retry.mjs is new and its exhaustion-path error shape is unverified at integration level
findings.body: scripts/lib/retry.mjs (203 new lines) provides the retry/backoff layer now consumed by glm-client.mjs. While tests/retry.test.mjs exists (352 lines — healthy ratio), the integration path through glm-client.mjs into the new bigmodel-errors.mjs dispatch is untested as a combined flow. If the retry layer wraps errors in a way the client or error dispatcher does not expect, the result is either (a) a swallowed error producing a silent undefined return, or (b) an unhandled rejection that crashes the plugin host. The risk is elevated because this is the network boundary where real failures are most frequent.
findings.recommendation: Add an integration-level test that exercises the full glm-client → retry → bigmodel-errors path with a mock server returning retryable then terminal errors, asserting that the final error shape matches what callers expect. Verify the error is propagated (not swallowed) and that any error code/message classification is preserved through the retry layer.

next_steps: Run `grep -rn 'process\.mjs' scripts/ commands/` from the repo root to confirm no surviving imports reference the deleted module.
Verify that tracked-jobs.mjs cleanup runs correctly after SIGTERM/SIGINT by adding a signal-handler test or manual verification.
Add a single integration test exercising glm-client with a retryable-then-terminal error sequence to confirm error propagation through retry.mjs and bigmodel-errors.mjs.
Consider adding a pre-push hook or CI gate that fails if any import statement references a file that does not exist in the working tree — this would have caught issue [redacted] automatically.
